zh.Davenport, Cheshire, England: 英國英格蘭爾達文波特
zh.Cransley, Northamptonshire, England: 英國諾薩普頓史爾可藍斯利
zh.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Cransley de Wheitrough是Peter Legh的女兒，於1295年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾可藍斯利。她於1315年與Thomas Davenport Of Wheltrough在英國爾達文波特結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1355年在英國英格蘭爾達文波特享年60歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Cransley de Wheitrough是Peter Legh的女儿，于1295年出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾可藍斯利。她于1315年与Thomas Davenport Of Wheltrough在英国爾達文波特结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1355年在英国英格兰爾達文波特享年60岁。

nameMeanings.name: Cransley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Cranleigh in Surrey or either of the places called Cranley, in Suffolk and Lincolnshire.
Irish: from Ó Crónghaile ‘descendant of Crónghal’, a personal name composed of crón ‘brown’ and gal ‘valor’.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Cransley de Wheitrough was born in 1295, in Cransley, Northamptonshire, England as the daughter of Peter Legh. She married Thomas Davenport Of Wheltrough in 1315, in Davenport, Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1355, in Davenport, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 60.

fr.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Cransley de Wheitrough est née en 1295, à Cransley, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, fille de Peter Legh. Elle a épousé Thomas Davenport Of Wheltrough en 1315, à Davenport, Cheshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1355, à Davenport, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 60 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Cransley de Wheitrough wurde 1295 in Cransley, Northamptonshire, England, als Tochter von Peter Legh geboren. Sie hat 1315 in Davenport, Cheshire, England Thomas Davenport Of Wheltrough geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1355 im Alter von 60 Jahren in Davenport, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Cransley de Wheitrough è nata il nel 1295 nel Cransley, Northamptonshire, England come figlia di Peter Legh. Lei sposato Thomas Davenport Of Wheltrough nel 1315, nel Davenport, Cheshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1355, nel Davenport, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 60 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Cransley de Wheitroughは1295年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアクランスリーでPeter Leghの娘として生まれた。1315年にイギリスチェシュアイアダベンプオルトでThomas Davenport Of Wheltroughと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1355年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアダベンプオルトで60歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Cransley de Wheitrough(은)는 1295년 영국 노르섬프토실르 크랜슬리에서 Peter Legh의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1315년 영국 체실르 대븐포트에서 Thomas Davenport Of Wheltrough(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1355년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 대븐포트에서 60세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Cransley de Wheitrough nasceu em 1295, em Cransley, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra filha de Peter Legh. Ela casou-se com Thomas Davenport Of Wheltrough em 1315, em Davenport, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1355, em Davenport, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 60 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Cransley de Wheitrough родилась в 1295 г. в местности крансли, нортамптоншир, Англия. Её отца звали Peter Legh. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Davenport Of Wheltrough в 1315 г. в местности давенпорт, чешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1355 г. в местности давенпорт, чешир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 60 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Cransley de Wheitrough nació en 1295, en Cransley, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra como hija de Peter Legh. Se casó con Thomas Davenport Of Wheltrough en 1315, en Davenport, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1355, en Davenport, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 60 años.
